misconceptions.display_name: Keeps the original limits after substituting
misconceptions.description: In a definite integral, substitutes u = g(x) but keeps the original x-limits instead of converting them to u-limits (or forgetting to back-substitute). The limits must be expressed in the new variable u before evaluating.
trigger_phrases: integrate from 0 to pi/4 in u

misconceptions.display_name: Swaps dx for du without computing du
misconceptions.description: Replaces the variable x by u but treats dx as if it were du, never computing du = g'(x) dx. The differential must be converted too, or the constant factor is lost.

misconceptions.display_name: Leaves the answer in terms of u
misconceptions.description: Finishes the integral in u and reports u^6/6 + C without back-substituting u = g(x), leaving the antiderivative in the wrong variable.
trigger_phrases: leave it in terms of u
